title: Option<Option<String>> for partial-update fields in ChatPresenceEvent is the correct three-state encoding
description: ChatPresenceEvent.status_text and status_emoji use Option<Option<String>> with a custom deserializer (deserialize_optional_nullable_string) to represent three distinct states: field absent (outer None = no change), field null (Some(None) = explicit clear), field string (Some(Some(s)) = update). This is the correct encoding for the spec's partial-update semantics: a presence push event updates only the fields that are present; absent fields must not overwrite cached state. A future contributor might suggest simplifying to Option<String> (treating null and absent as equivalent) or flattening to String with a sentinel value. Option<String> silently conflates 'no change' with 'clear', which would incorrectly wipe cached status when a presence update arrives that does not touch those fields. The three-state semantics are tested with fixture-backed tests: test_chat_presence_event_null_clears_status, test_chat_presence_event_absent_status_means_no_change, and test_deserialize_optional_nullable_string_rejects_non_string.
